ulf_hook: The Westshore calls to the exile. A beast with the strength of stars falls to the earth. The stone-marrow yields to the sharp edge of the flint.
grandpa_legend: The scent of charred meat from the new cooking stones always settles the spirit after a long trek through the brush. I followed the hum of the Golden Ones to their hidden hoards and marked the red-fruit patches that will keep us fed through the winter. My old bones carried me as far as the Westshore, where I looked upon the altar of the Forsaken and tested my mettle against a stream-dweller with the strength of stars. I took its head for the hall and found shelter among the Echoes of the Past, stitching rag-garments and shaping the stone-marrow into a proper axe. It’s a humble start, but a sharp blade and a warm bed make the wild feel a little more like home.
conrad_chronicle: The session focused on early-game infrastructure and exploration, beginning with the construction of a cooking station and the discovery of multiple beehives. The player mapped out an abandoned village and a raspberry patch while looting flint arrows and gold from ruined structures. Reaching the western coastline revealed a second Eikthyr altar and resulted in a combat encounter with a two-star neck, providing the first trophy. A new spawn point was established using a bed and basic shelter at a secondary exploration site. Technical progression was marked by repairing all equipment and crafting both rag trousers and a flint axe to improve combat effectiveness.
